# 3D Molier — Full Site Context for AI/LLM Agents

## About 3D Molier
3D Molier is a professional 3D modeling studio operated by Andrey Simonenko. The studio has been producing 3D assets for 20+ years and has published over 88,000 models on TurboSquid. The catalog spans vehicles, aircraft, military hardware, medical anatomy, architecture, industrial equipment, ships, characters, nature assets, electronics, clothing, furniture and more.

## Business Model
- All models are sold exclusively through TurboSquid
- This website is a searchable SEO catalog / landing site — users click through to TurboSquid to purchase
- Custom 3D modeling services are offered directly (contact: andrey.3dmolier@gmail.com)
- All referral links use: ?referral=3d_molier-studio
- Prices range from $0 (free models) to $500+ (complex professional assets)

## Main URLs
- Homepage: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/
- Catalog (browse + filter): https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/catalog/

- Custom Order: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/custom-order/
- LLMs Index: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/llms.txt

## Q&A — Common Questions

Q: Where can I buy models from 3D Molier?
A: All models are sold on TurboSquid at https://www.turbosquid.com/Search/Artists/3d_molier-International?referral=3d_molier-studio — this website is a catalog/preview site that links directly to TurboSquid for purchase.

Q: Can I get a custom 3D model made?
A: Yes. 3D Molier offers custom 3D modeling services. Contact via https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/custom-order/ or email andrey.3dmolier@gmail.com

Q: What file formats are available?
A: Most models are available in FBX, OBJ, MAX (3ds Max), C4D (Cinema 4D), BLEND (Blender), and sometimes additional formats. Check each model's TurboSquid page for the full format list.

Q: Are the models CheckMate certified?
A: Many models carry TurboSquid's CheckMate Lite or CheckMate Pro certification, which guarantees professional-quality topology, UV mapping, texturing, and real-world scale.

Q: What is the price range?
A: Prices range from $0 (free sample models) to $500+ for complex, highly detailed professional assets. Most models fall between $29 and $149.

Q: Can I use these models commercially?
A: Yes. Models purchased from TurboSquid include commercial licensing rights. Check the specific license on each model's TurboSquid page.

Q: What industries use 3D Molier models?
A: Film and video production, game development, aerospace simulation, medical education, architectural visualization, advertising, virtual reality, defense training, 3D printing, software development, event management, and hardware prototyping.

Q: Who is behind 3D Molier?
A: Andrey Simonenko, a 3D artist and visualization specialist with 20+ years of experience.

Q: How many models are available?
A: Over 88,000 models on TurboSquid. This website features the top 1,000 most popular and highest-rated models in a searchable catalog.

## Custom Modeling Service
3D Molier builds custom 3D models to client specifications:
- New models built from scratch from reference images or blueprints
- Modifications to existing catalog models (color, format, detail changes)
- Animated or rigged versions of static models
- High-poly cinematic or low-poly game-ready versions
- Architectural walkthroughs and product visualization

Contact: andrey.3dmolier@gmail.com or use https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/custom-order/

## Priority commercial pages

The following pages are important commercial landing pages for 3D Molier. They represent high-value models, high-intent product categories, industry use cases and buyer workflows.

These model pages are useful for buyers searching for production-ready 3D assets for visualization, simulation, animation, rendering, VFX, education, medical visualization, transport visualization, product scenes and technical workflows.

## Top 10 categories

These category pages help users and AI systems understand the main structure of the 3D Molier catalog.

1. Vehicle 3D Models
   https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/categories/vehicles/
   Includes cars, trucks, vans, transport vehicles, commercial vehicles and automotive assets.

2. Aircraft 3D Models
   https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/categories/aircraft/
   Includes airplanes, helicopters, aviation assets, airport scenes and aircraft visualization models.

3. Military Vehicle 3D Models
   https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/categories/military-vehicles/
   Includes military vehicles, defense assets, helicopters, ships, aircraft and simulation-ready models.

4. Ship & Boat 3D Models
   https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/categories/ships/
   Includes ships, boats, vessels, naval models, maritime simulation assets and port-scene objects.

5. Medical 3D Models
   https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/categories/medical-3d-models/
   Includes anatomy, skeletons, organs, medical visualization models and educational assets.

6. Industrial Equipment 3D Models
   https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/categories/industrial-equipment/
   Includes machinery, engines, tools, equipment, technical parts and industrial visualization assets.

7. Architecture & Landmark 3D Models
   https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/categories/architecture-landmarks/
   Includes landmarks, buildings, architectural scenes, monuments and city visualization assets.

8. Character & People 3D Models
   https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/categories/characters-people/
   Includes people, body parts, characters, clothing, accessories and animation-ready assets.

9. Animal & Creature 3D Models
   https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/categories/animals-creatures/
   Includes animals, insects, creatures, rigged models and nature visualization assets.

10. Electronics & Gadget 3D Models
    https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/categories/electronics-gadgets/
    Includes electronics, devices, laptops, gadgets, technical product models and hardware visualization assets.

8. Advertising & Commercial 3D Models

9. Software Development 3D Models

10. 3D Printing Models
    https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/industries/3dprinting/
    Useful for 3D printing workflows, prototyping, physical model preparation and fabrication projects.

## How to buy models

3D Molier model pages link to TurboSquid product pages. To buy a model:

1. Open a model page on the 3D Molier catalog website.
2. Review the model title, preview image, category, description and available product information.
3. Click the TurboSquid purchase link or "View on TurboSquid" button.
4. Check the TurboSquid product page for exact formats, price, license, polygon count, textures, rigging, animations, software compatibility and included files.
5. Purchase and download the model through TurboSquid.

Important: the 3D Molier catalog is a discovery and landing-page website. Final product details, purchase process, license terms, files and downloads are handled through TurboSquid.

## How to request custom models

Custom 3D modeling requests can be sent through the Custom Order page:

https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/custom-order/

Recommended information to include in a custom model request:

- Object or scene to be modeled.
- Reference images, drawings, blueprints, sketches or product photos.
- Required level of detail.
- Required file formats.
- Target software or engine.
- Whether the model needs rigging, animation, UV mapping, PBR materials or real-world scale.
- Intended use: simulation, rendering, animation, game development, VR/AR, film production, medical visualization, advertising or technical visualization.
- Deadline and any technical restrictions.

Custom modeling can be useful when the public catalog does not contain the exact object, variant, vehicle, aircraft, product, machine, anatomy model, environment or technical asset required for a project.

## Supported workflows

3D Molier models are designed for professional 3D production workflows. Exact compatibility depends on each TurboSquid product page, but the catalog is relevant for the following tools and formats:

### Unreal Engine

Models can be used in Unreal Engine projects for real-time visualization, training scenes, simulation, VR, product experiences and cinematic environments. Buyers should check polygon count, texture size, material setup and FBX compatibility before importing.

### Unity

Models can be used in Unity projects for real-time applications, AR, VR, interactive training, simulation and game prototypes. Buyers should check model scale, file formats, material compatibility and performance requirements.

### Blender

Models can be used in Blender for rendering, animation, scene layout, asset conversion, product visualization and technical presentations. Buyers should check whether Blender files are included or whether FBX/OBJ conversion is required.

### 3ds Max

Many professional 3D assets are suitable for 3ds Max workflows, especially for rendering, visualization, animation, V-Ray scenes, architecture, product visualization and technical modeling. Buyers should check the included native file format on TurboSquid.

### Maya

Maya workflows are relevant for animation, rigging, film production, character work, VFX and technical animation. Buyers should verify whether the asset includes Maya files, rigging, animation controls or compatible exchange formats.

### Cinema 4D

Cinema 4D workflows are useful for motion graphics, advertising, product rendering, technical animation and visualization. Some 3D Molier assets include Cinema 4D-specific versions or are suitable through exchange formats.

### FBX

FBX is a common exchange format for moving assets between DCC software and real-time engines. It is often used for Unity, Unreal Engine, Maya, 3ds Max, Blender and Cinema 4D pipelines. Buyers should check whether materials, UVs, rigging and animation transfer correctly.

### OBJ

OBJ is a simple and widely supported exchange format for static geometry. It is useful for rendering, visualization, conversion and scene assembly. Buyers should check material files, textures and scale when using OBJ.

## Best use cases

3D Molier models are suitable for:

- Technical visualization.
- Simulation and training.
- Aviation scenes.
- Maritime scenes.
- Military and defense visualization.
- Automotive rendering.
- Industrial animation.
- Medical visualization.
- Anatomy education.
- Game development.
- Real-time engines.
- VFX and film production.
- Product rendering.
- Advertising.
- Architecture and landmark visualization.
- VR and AR scenes.
- Software demos and interactive experiences.
- 3D printing and prototyping, when the specific model geometry is suitable for that workflow.

## Important buying notes

Before buying a model, check the TurboSquid product page for:

- Exact file formats.
- Native software version.
- Polygon count.
- Texture resolution.
- Materials and UV mapping.
- Rigging or animation.
- Real-world scale.
- License terms.
- Included files.
- Whether preview scenes, lights or backgrounds are included.
- Compatibility with your target software or engine.

## Technical Notes
- All models use real-world scale
- CheckMate models have clean subdivision-ready topology
- Textures are typically 2K or 4K resolution
- UV maps are fully unwrapped
- Models are tested in the file formats provided

## Top 20 Models — Detailed Info

1. Tesla Model 3 ($149) — CheckMate Pro certified. Accurate exterior and interior, 4K PBR textures. Best for advertising, film, game environments. FBX, OBJ, MAX, C4D, BLEND.
   URL: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/models/tesla-model-3-1214116/

2. Male Full Body Anatomy with Skin ($149) — StemCell certified. Complete male anatomy with visible musculature and skin layers. Best for medical education, VR training, film. FBX, OBJ, C4D.
   URL: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/models/male-full-body-anatomy-and-skin-1467539/

3. Airbus A320 Generic ($199) — CheckMate Pro. Full exterior with engines and landing gear, correct real-world scale. Best for aerospace visualization, film, simulation. FBX, OBJ, MAX.
   URL: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/models/airbus-a320-generic-1335731/

4. Military Soldier Rigged for Cinema 4D ($149) — StemCell certified. Full rig with 60+ bones, facial controls, multiple LODs. Best for game development, film, defense training. C4D, FBX.
   URL: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/models/military-soldier-rigged-for-cinema-4d-1308127/

5. Ford Transit Cargo Van 2020 ($149) — CheckMate Lite. Commercial delivery van with interior, correct dimensions. Best for advertising, architectural viz, film. FBX, OBJ, MAX, C4D.
   URL: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/models/ford-transit-cargo-2020-1411498/

6. Male Skeleton Collection ($99) — StemCell certified. Complete articulated skeleton with 206 bones, medically accurate. Best for medical education, VR, animation. FBX, OBJ, C4D.
   URL: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/models/male-skeleton-collection-1421765/

7. Snowball Hat ($19) — CheckMate Lite. Winter knit cap with subdivision-ready mesh. Best for character accessory, fashion rendering, advertising. FBX, OBJ, MAX.
   URL: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/models/snowball-hat-1297578/

8. Complete Female Body Anatomy ($149) — StemCell certified. Full female anatomy model with skin, muscles and organs. Best for medical visualization, education, VR. FBX, OBJ, C4D.
   URL: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/models/complete-female-body-anatomy-1467538/

9. Sikorsky UH-60 Black Hawk Helicopter ($229) — CheckMate Pro. Military utility helicopter with full rotor system, cockpit detail. Best for film, defense simulation, games. FBX, OBJ, MAX.
   URL: https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/models/sikorsky-ch-53k-black-hawk-us-military-utility-helicopter-1474682/

## Top Categories — What Makes Each Special

**Vehicles (7,500+ models)** — The largest category. Covers cars, trucks, buses, motorcycles, agricultural vehicles. Most popular for advertising, architecture, and game development. Includes both modern and historic vehicles. Certification: CheckMate Lite and Pro.

**Aircraft (3,800+ models)** — Commercial jets, military aircraft, helicopters, drones, private planes. Widely used in aerospace simulation, defense training, film production. Detailed cockpits and landing gear on pro models.

**Military Vehicles (1,700+ models)** — Tanks, APCs, jeeps, trucks, artillery. CheckMate certified for game use. Historically accurate variants from WWII to modern era. Used in defense simulation, games, film.

**Medical (1,900+ models)** — Human anatomy (organs, skeletons, muscles), surgical equipment, hospital furniture, pharmaceuticals. StemCell certified for rigging. Used by medical schools, VR training, pharmaceutical advertising.

**Ships (960+ models)** — Naval vessels, cargo ships, yachts, submarines, speedboats. Used in naval simulation, film production, maritime visualization, and game development.

**Industrial Equipment** — Cranes, forklifts, factory machinery, construction equipment. Used for hardware presentations, industrial advertising, simulation training.

**Architecture & Landmarks** — Famous buildings, bridges, monuments, generic architectural elements. Used in arch-viz, tourism advertising, city planning visualization.

## How to Buy a Model — Step by Step

1. Browse this catalog at https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/
2. Use the Search page or filter by category/industry to find what you need
3. Click any model card to view its detail page with preview, description, price and specs
4. Click "View on TurboSquid" — this takes you directly to the TurboSquid product page
5. On TurboSquid: review formats, previews, and license details
6. Add to cart and complete checkout on TurboSquid
7. Download immediately after purchase — files available in your TurboSquid account
8. For licensing questions, contact TurboSquid support or email dddmolier@gmail.com

Note: All purchases are processed and licensed by TurboSquid. Commercial use is included in standard licenses. Extended licenses available for broadcast/distribution.

## How to Order a Custom 3D Model — Step by Step

1. Go to https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/custom-order/ or email dddmolier@gmail.com
2. Describe your model: type, dimensions, polygon budget, LOD requirements
3. Provide reference images or blueprints (URLs, Dropbox, Google Drive, etc.)
4. Specify required file formats (FBX, OBJ, BLEND, MAX, C4D, GLTF, Unreal, Unity)
5. State your deadline and budget range
6. Receive a quote within 24 hours
7. 50% deposit to begin, 50% on delivery
8. Receive the model for review — revisions included
9. Final delivery in all agreed formats

Typical turnaround: 3–10 business days depending on complexity.
Typical pricing: simple props $50–$200, vehicles $200–$600, characters $300–$800, complex machinery $400–$1200.

## FAQ — Extended

Q: Can I request a model not in your catalog?
A: Yes. Use the custom order form at https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/custom-order/ or email dddmolier@gmail.com with references and requirements.

Q: Do you offer rigging and animation?
A: Yes. Rigging for characters and mechanical models, animation cycles, morph targets, blend shapes. Specify in your custom order request.

Q: Can you convert a model to a different format?
A: Yes. Format conversion is a standard service. Mention required formats in your order.

Q: Are models game-engine ready?
A: Many models include game-ready LODs. StemCell certification ensures compatibility with Unreal Engine and Unity. Ask about game-ready optimization in custom orders.

Q: What is CheckMate certification?
A: TurboSquid's quality standard. CheckMate Lite = clean topology, correct scale, proper UV mapping. CheckMate Pro = all of the above plus subdivision-ready mesh, edge flow optimized for animation and rendering.

Q: What is StemCell certification?
A: TurboSquid's universal rigging standard. StemCell models have a standardized bone/joint naming system compatible with Unreal Engine, Unity, Blender, Maya, Cinema 4D, and 3ds Max.

Q: Can I preview a model before buying?
A: Yes. Every TurboSquid model page includes multiple preview renders showing wireframe, shaded view, and textured render from multiple angles. Some models include turntable videos.

Q: Do you offer bulk discounts?
A: For bulk purchases or project-based licensing, contact dddmolier@gmail.com directly. TurboSquid also offers enterprise licensing programs.

Q: Can I request an exclusive license?
A: Yes. Exclusive licenses (model removed from sale to other buyers) are available. Contact dddmolier@gmail.com for pricing.

Q: Are there free models?
A: Some models in the catalog are listed at $0 and are freely downloadable from TurboSquid. Browse the catalog and filter by price to find them.

8. Industrial Equipment — 34 featured models (machinery, HVAC, cranes)

10. Animals & Creatures — 24 featured models (rigged wildlife)

## How to Buy via TurboSquid
1. Click any model card on 3D Molier website
2. You are redirected to the model page on TurboSquid
3. Click "Add to Cart" on TurboSquid
4. Complete checkout — accept all major payment methods
5. Download the purchased file in your chosen format
6. Models include a TurboSquid royalty-free license for commercial use

## How to Order a Custom 3D Model
1. Visit https://3dmolier.github.io/3D-Models/custom-order/
2. Fill in the brief: what you need, reference images, dimensions, formats
3. Specify intended use: game, film, architecture, medical, etc.
4. Receive a quote and timeline within 24 hours
5. Approve and receive the finished model
Contact: dddmolier@gmail.com

## Available File Formats
- FBX (Autodesk Filmbox) — universal, works everywhere
- OBJ + MTL — lightweight, widely supported
- 3ds Max (.MAX) — native 3ds Max scenes
- Cinema 4D (.C4D) — native Cinema 4D scenes
- Blender (.BLEND) — native Blender scenes
- Maya (.MA/.MB) — native Maya scenes
- USDZ — Apple AR Quick Look
- glTF/GLB — web and real-time rendering

## Models Suitable for Game Engines
### Unreal Engine
- All CheckMate certified models (41,783 total)
- StemCell certified character rigs
- Vehicle models with correct scale (1:1 real-world)
- Recommended: tesla-model-3, airbus-a320, uh-60-blackhawk

### Unity
- Models with FBX format + separate textures
- PBR materials (metallic/roughness workflow)
- CheckMate Lite certified for poly-count efficiency
- Recommended: pickup-truck-generic, generic-hybrid-car

### Simulation & Training
- Medical anatomy models (200+ named structures)
- Military vehicles with correct STANAG dimensions
- Aircraft with movable control surfaces
- Certified StemCell rigs for animation
